description:
Adjutant Vask's office at the Bureau of Adjustment station. The contract board is here, the ledger is here, and Vask is here. When you've earned a place on the roster (signing_on completed), this is where you come back between contracts: ask for work, take work, turn in work, get paid. Nothing else happens here unless the player drives it — Vask is at her desk all hours, the room is otherwise empty.

Triggers:
1. On encounter start (bureau_intro — aigm): the AIGM opens with the brief beat that the player has returned to Vask's office and the contract board is waiting. Should NOT replay the full signing_on backstory; the player has been here before.
2. When the player completes a contract elsewhere and returns (debrief_aigm — aigm): only fires when `mission_complete` is set and `mission_reward_claimed` is unset, so the AIGM knows to nudge the player toward Vask for the turn-in.
customIntroduction: Vask's office is exactly as you left it. A plank desk piled with rolled-up contracts, an oil lamp turned low, the door at your back, and Vask herself behind it — she does not look up when you enter, but she has marked the door without breaking from her ledger. The contract board on the wall has three new pins since you saw it last.
customContext:
BUREAU OFFICE — HUB ENCOUNTER. This is the contract-taking hub between field missions. Vask is the only NPC. The player came here to ASK FOR A NEW CONTRACT, TURN IN A FINISHED CONTRACT, or just CHAT. The room has no enemies and no tension. Treat it as a quiet workplace.

VASK'S STATE depends on world flags the engine sets — the conversation tree (`bureau_office_chat`) reads them directly. Do not narrate the contract content yourself — the conversation system handles the offer, the turn-in, and the reward; you provide flavour beats around the edges (Vask sets down her pen, takes the ledger, hands over a coin pouch, etc.).

IF the player asks for a contract: a short Vask line, then defer to the conversation system. IF the player has a finished contract and walks in: the AIGM can narrate Vask noting his return ("You're back. Show me the seal.") and then defer to the turn-in flow.

NO COMBAT in this room. If the player tries to attack Vask, narrate her standing up, hand on the bell-pull that summons every other regular on duty, and tell the player plainly that violence here ends the commission.
objective: Take a contract. Run it. Come back. Get paid.

then.message: Open at Vask's office in Harrowgate station — quiet, lamplit, after-hours feel. The player has come to take a contract. Vask is behind her desk; she will not start the offer herself — she expects to be asked. Single short beat, then wait.

The player has just returned from a finished contract. Vask looks up from the ledger this time — she's been expecting them. Narrate her acknowledging the return with the dry, plain affect she always uses ("You came back. Show me what you've got.") and then defer to the conversation system for the turn-in. Do not name the reward yourself.
Vask has just paid the contract out. A clean beat — coin counted onto the desk, the entry inked into the ledger. The player can ask for another contract or step away.
